rationale_top3: Exceptional entertainment facilities with resort-style heated pool complex featuring two waterslides that keep children entertained for hours, complemented by jumping pillow, pedal karts, and daily kids activities coordinated by enthusiastic staff Outstanding cleanliness standards consistently described as 'spotless,' 'immaculate,' and 'the cleanest park' with amenities maintained to exceptional standards throughout the day, backed by dedicated cleaning staff Prime waterfront location providing direct access to coastal walks and beaches, plus walking distance to major supermarkets, cafes, restaurants and Caloundra town centre, combining natural beauty with urban convenience
rationale_honourable: Strong family loyalty with guests returning annually for 3-12+ years and rebooking before departure, demonstrating exceptional satisfaction and memorable experiences Spacious premium sites with well-maintained artificial grass and drive-through options, though site sizing varies across the property Beautiful natural setting with salt-water creek, ocean views from premium cabins, and easy access to regional attractions including Australia Zoo and Aussie World
water_fun: Resort-style heated pool with two waterslides
kids_play: Jumping pillow, pedal karts, daily activities
pet_detail: Dog-friendly with $10 per night fee, no dedicated dog facilities such as wash stations or off-leash areas mentioned
best_for: Families with young children seeking a resort-style holiday park experience with exceptional pool facilities, modern amenities, and convenient location near beaches and shops
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Caloundra Waterfront Holiday Park
SCORE: 88/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 440

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Reviews consistently praise the "spectacular" heated pool complex with waterslides that keep kids entertained for hours, jumping pillow, pedal karts and go-karts, daily kids activities including discos and bingo, and the kids activities coordinator Nick who is "amazing with kids." The onsite Tripcony Traders restaurant with poolside service and Tom & Andy's coffee van add to the resort-style entertainment experience.

Nature (17/20) — The waterfront location provides "stunning walks along the coast," direct access to beaches and coastal pathways through back gates, water views from premium cabins, and a salt-water creek running through the property. Families appreciate the proximity to beaches, the esplanade walk linking multiple beaches, and the "beautiful scenic view of the ocean by the pool."

Cleanliness (15/15) — Overwhelmingly described as "spotless," "immaculate," and "the cleanest park we have stayed at." Reviewers specifically mention showers, toilets, and camp kitchen are "always spotless," amenities "sparkling clean no matter the time of day," and cleaning staff are called out by name for exceptional work. The park is "beautifully maintained" with manicured grounds and gardens.

Value (11/15) — While facilities are described as "worth every cent" and meals "reasonably priced," many reviewers note the park is "expensive," "pricey," and "not for the budget conscience" at $100+ per night for powered sites and $350+ for cabins during peak times. Some felt pricing was inconsistent or increased significantly year-over-year, though most concluded "you get what you pay for."

Site Size (8/10) — Sites described as "adequate but not huge," "spacious," and "well maintained" with artificial grass. Premium sites offer "the right size of large to spread out," though some standard sites are "tight" for larger vans, with caravans hanging over roads. Drive-through sites praised, but size varies significantly across the park.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional loyalty with families returning annually for 3-12+ years, many rebook before leaving, and consistently recommend to others. Described as "favourite place to stay," "best holiday park," and one family has made it a yearly birthday tradition. Very high recommendation rate despite premium pricing.

Location (9/10) — Perfect for families: 2-minute walk to Coles and Woolworths, walking distance to Caloundra town centre, beaches, cafes and restaurants, and RSL. Easy access to Australia Zoo, Aussie World, Eumundi Markets. Only drawback is road noise on sites backing the highway, though proximity to amenities typically compensates.

WHY NOT 100:
Pricing is significantly higher than comparable parks ($100-350+ per night), with some guests feeling pricing inconsistencies and year-over-year increases ($800 increase for one regular) make it "out of touch with caravan affordability." While facilities justify premium rates for many, value-conscious families may find better options elsewhere.

Site size variability creates inconsistent experiences, with some sites described as "TINY" with "mud after rain," vans overhanging roads, and insufficient space between neighbours, while premium sites are spacious. Traffic noise affects sites along the main road significantly, requiring air conditioning to block sound at night.

Kids entertainment infrastructure has gaps including no proper playground equipment (only jumping pillow), pool closes at 6:30pm which families found limiting, and some reviewers noted lack of activities during non-peak periods. The jumping pillow was described as "terrible" by one family, and larger unsupervised children can dominate shared play spaces.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Simply the Best Caravan park in Queensland", "the cleanest campground I have stayed at", "feels like staying at an all inclusive holiday resort", "pool and slides provide hours of fun", "amenities spotless and sparkling clean", "staff went above and beyond", "real family memories are made", "resort styling living"

rationale_top3: Exceptional family entertainment facilities including heated pool with water slides, jumping pillow, mini golf, pedal karts, and daily organized activities such as kids' discos, movie nights, archery, and wildlife shows that keep children engaged throughout their stay. Beautiful Maroochy River frontage providing natural setting with fishing, kayaking, crabbing from the pontoon, and stunning sunset views, combined with extremely spacious powered sites described as the biggest guests have experienced. Resort-style experience with luxury glamping safari tents featuring full bathrooms and air conditioning, poolside cabana bookings, food service, and consistently praised staff who go above and beyond to create a welcoming family atmosphere.
rationale_honourable: Convenient Sunshine Coast location just 15 minutes from major attractions like the Big Pineapple and Sea Life Aquarium, with easy highway access for day trips to beaches and Noosa, while maintaining well-maintained facilities that most guests found clean and spotless.
water_fun: Heated pool with two slides and splash pool
kids_play: Playground, jumping pillow, mini golf, pedal karts
pet_detail: Dog friendly with dogs permitted in designated areas including ground floor of bar/bistro area on leash
best_for: Families with multiple children who want a full resort experience without leaving the campground, particularly those who enjoy water activities and organized kids' entertainment alongside natural river access
executive_summary:
PARK: Ingenia Holidays Rivershore
SCORE: 86/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-22
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 302

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Reviews consistently praised the heated pool with water slides as "ridiculously fun," the jumping pillow, mini golf, pedal karts, and daily organized activities including kids' discos, movie nights, archery, wildlife shows, and scavenger hunts. Families noted "so much to do with the kids" and children "never stopped smiling." The pool area is described as "resort style" with cabanas available for booking and poolside food service.

Nature (17/20) — The park sits on the Maroochy River with "stunning location right next to the river" and "beautiful big grassy area" for kids to play. Guests enjoyed fishing from the pontoon, kayaking, crabbing, and "beautiful views of sunsets and sunrise." The natural riverfront setting is described as "magical" though some noted it's not directly on a beach and mosquitoes can be an issue near the water.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Most reviews praised "spotless" facilities with "amenities always clean and well maintained" and bathrooms "kept clean." Several families noted daily cleaning of pool areas and regular bin emptying. However, some recent reviews mentioned cleanliness standards had "lapsed" with issues like cobwebs, weeds near cabanas, and bathrooms needing more frequent attention during busy periods. A few glamping tents needed "DEEP clean" with the floor feeling "grimey."

Value (12/15) — Pricing received mixed feedback. Many felt it was "great value for money" with "reasonably priced" food and drinks, particularly appreciating included activities. However, several reviews noted it as "very expensive" at nearly $200-250 per night for powered sites and $580 per night for glamping tents. Some felt "ripped off" given the pricing, though many still felt facilities justified the cost. Food described as "slightly on the higher price range" but acceptable.

Site Size (9/10) — Powered sites consistently praised as "huge," "extremely spacious," and "the biggest site I've ever stated at." Families noted sites could "fit another caravan and car" and "even with huge caravans on either side we didn't feel cramped." Unpowered riverfront sites described as "HUGE and grass beautifully kept." Minor deductions for some roads being "tight to maneuver through" and glamping tents built "very close on either sides."

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with phrases like "we will definitely be back," "absolute dream come true," "couldn't have been more pleased," and "one of the best parks we've stayed at." Many families book return visits and describe it as their "favourite caravan park." Some noted it as a "yearly visit for us." Staff praised as "amazing," "friendly," and "go above and beyond." Minor issues with occasional rude reception staff and inconsistent service quality.

Location (8/10) — Positioned centrally on the Sunshine Coast, "15 mins from tourist activities such as the big pineapple and sealife aquarium" and close to beaches, shops (IGA 4km), and attractions. Easy highway access for day trips to Noosa. However, several reviews mentioned significant road noise from the adjacent busy David Low Way, particularly at night with traffic described as "very busy road" and "extreme traffic noise." Not within walking distance to beaches or town.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Road noise from the adjacent David Low Way significantly impacted sleep quality for multiple guests, with some describing "extreme traffic noise all night" that "destroyed all the beauty" and caused them to cancel stays early.

2. Cleanliness standards have become inconsistent, particularly during peak periods, with recent reviews noting bathrooms not cleaned daily, showers needing "good scrub," glamping tents requiring deep cleaning, and maintenance issues like weeds, cobwebs, and blood on toilet doors remaining for days.

3. Overcrowding during school holidays and weekends severely impacts guest experience, with day visitors (paying $20 entry) taking over pool cabanas and facilities, making it "too busy and dangerous" for young children, forcing paying guests to leave pool areas despite paying $250-580 per night.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"One of the best holiday parks ever," "absolute paradise for families," "resort style pool," "safari tent was pure luxury," "staff go above and beyond," "extreme traffic noise all night," "pool overcrowded with day guests," "facilities have become quite run down," "kids never stopped smiling," "we compared it to being on a cruise ship," "heated pool perfect for cooler nights," "sites are huge"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards with amenities cleaned twice daily and described as 'spotless' across 416 reviews, earning perfect 15/15 cleanliness score Outstanding natural riverfront setting with direct access to Maroochy River walking/cycling paths, fishing spots, and beautiful tropical gardens earning 16/20 nature score Strong entertainment program for young families including jumping pillow, mini golf, school holiday activities, Wednesday pancake breakfasts, and evening campfires with marshmallows earning 14/20
rationale_honourable: The park's friendly, helpful staff consistently go 'above and beyond' with personalized service, while unique conveniences like the beloved 'marriage saver' tractor parking assistance create a welcoming community atmosphere that generates exceptional repeat visitation and loyal guests returning for 7+ years
water_fun: Small but clean pool with shade
kids_play: Jumping pillow, putt putt, playground activities
pet_detail: Pet friendly with $10 per night fee; designated smoking area outside park; some sites are pet-free zones
best_for: Families with young children who prioritize cleanliness, friendly service, and a well-maintained environment over entertainment variety and site spaciousness
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Maroochy River
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 416

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews consistently mention jumping pillow, mini golf, small pool, and playground as core attractions. School holiday activities including movie nights, disco, reptile shows, science shows, daily kids programs, and visiting food trucks received strong praise. Wednesday pancake breakfasts and evening campfires with marshmallows created memorable experiences. However, multiple families noted limited entertainment variety for older children/teens, with several calling the pool "very small" and facilities "not much for big kids to do" compared to other BIG4 parks.

Nature (16/20) — The park sits directly across from the Maroochy River with excellent walking/cycling paths, fishing access, and sunset viewing opportunities. Reviewers loved the "beautiful river walks," herb gardens throughout the park, and tropical garden atmosphere with "manicured lawns, flowers and fresh herbs." However, the park itself is described as "small" and the natural setting is significantly compromised by being situated between the Sunshine Coast Motorway and busy arterial roads, with constant traffic noise diminishing the nature experience.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Exceptional scores across hundreds of reviews. Described as "cleanest park we've ever been to," "immaculately maintained," "spotless amenities," and "amenities cleaned twice daily." Staff named individually (Lisa, Emily, Savannah) for outstanding cleaning work. Grounds, toilets, showers, camp kitchens, and sites all received consistent 5-star cleanliness ratings with maintenance staff visibly working throughout the day.

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing. Many found it "reasonable" and "good value" with quality facilities. However, several reviews complained about high prices: "$350 for a cabin ridiculous," "expensive for what it was," "definitely not worth the money we paid," and "way too expensive for what you actually get." The $10 per night pet fee drew specific criticism as a "money gouging rip off." Peak season pricing and powered site costs were frequent pain points.

Site Size (8/10) — Sites received mixed reviews. Many praised "nice roomy sites," "good size caravan sites," "spacious" with artificial turf. However, multiple reviews noted "very tight getting in with larger vans," "sites are very close together," "squeezed in that close," and "vans like 1.5 meters either side of you." The tractor "marriage saver" service exists specifically because sites are tight for reversing, though this service received overwhelming praise.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very high repeat visitation with phrases like "we keep coming back," "this is our favourite caravan park," "will definitely be back," and "7 years enjoying one of the best managed parks." Staff consistently praised for friendliness and going "above and beyond." However, some negative experiences included complaints about management, safety concerns, ant problems in cabins, and several guests leaving early due to noise issues.

Location (5/10) — The park's location receives the most polarized feedback. Positives include being "central to everything," "close to shops, beaches and holiday attractions," with IGA, pub, and Flume cafe within walking distance, and "great location for getting around the Sunshine Coast." However, the proximity to the Sunshine Coast Motorway is a major detractor: "very very loud traffic noise," "right by the motorway SO noisy," "road noise is like staying in a breakdown lane," "trucks vibrated the van," and "traffic noise was LOUD until 10pm and started again around 4:30am." Multiple families left early specifically due to noise.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Significant motorway and arterial road noise impacts many sites, with numerous families reporting poor sleep and some leaving early due to constant traffic sounds from 4:30am onwards.
2. Limited entertainment variety for older children and teens, with a notably small pool that becomes overcrowded during busy periods and insufficient activities compared to other BIG4 parks.
3. Premium pricing doesn't align with value expectations for some families, particularly given the small pool, limited facilities, tight sites, and additional pet fees that reviewers found excessive.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"cleanest park we've ever stayed in", "marriage saver tractor is the BEST thing", "staff super friendly and helpful", "very very loud traffic noise from motorway", "spotless amenities", "small pool", "immaculately maintained", "Wednesday pancake breakfast", "sites very close together", "jumping pillow for the kids", "right on Maroochy River", "expensive for what it was"

rationale_top3: Outstanding natural setting with nature score of 19/20, indicating excellent coastal environment and natural beauty Strong entertainment facilities scoring 14/20, suggesting good amenities and activities for families High cleanliness standards at 13/20, demonstrating well-maintained facilities and grounds
rationale_honourable: Excellent location score of 10/10, positioned at Dicky Beach on the Sunshine Coast with premium beach access Good value for money at 11/20, offering reasonable pricing for the coastal location and facilities provided

kids_play: Solid entertainment facilities scoring 14/20 suggest good playground equipment and children's activities suitable for family holiday experiences

rationale_top3: Outstanding natural environment with direct access to both patrolled surf beach and calm Maroochy River inlet, consistently praised as 'best of both worlds' with safe swimming for children Exceptional location described as 'you can't beat' with walking distance to Cotton Tree village, cafes, restaurants, shops, and Maroochydore CBD Generally well-maintained facilities with many recent reviews noting 'spotless' and 'impeccably clean' amenities when not at peak capacity
rationale_honourable: Cotton Tree Holiday Park earns Silver classification primarily for its exceptional beachfront location between the Maroochy River inlet and patrolled surf beach. While the natural setting is world-class and facilities are generally well-maintained, the park is let down by cramped sites, inconsistent customer service, premium pricing that doesn't match amenities offered, and lack of on-site entertainment facilities like pools or playgrounds that families expect at this price point.
water_fun: River inlet and patrolled beach access only
kids_play: No on-site playground or kids entertainment facilities

executive_summary:
PARK: Cotton Tree Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-11
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 257

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews consistently note complete absence of on-site entertainment facilities. No pool, no playground, no kids club, no organized activities. Multiple families specifically mention "no pool or other entertainment" and "Cotton tree have not much to offer except a beach." Park relies entirely on natural beach/river environment for entertainment.

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural environment with direct access to both patrolled surf beach and calm Maroochy River inlet. Reviews praise "best of both worlds, access to the Maroochydore river and the ocean," "calm waters great for kids," "amazing sunrises at your doorstep," and "between the river and the beach." Only minor deduction for beach erosion mentioned in some reviews.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Mixed feedback with recent reviews showing improvement. Positive comments include "amenities are spotless," "impeccably clean," "cleanest caravan park toilet I ever been," and "very clean facilities." However, multiple peak-season reviews mention "amenities were gross," "beyond filth," "constantly running out of toilet paper," and "dump points completely full for days." Cleanliness appears highly dependent on occupancy levels.

Value (8/15) — Significant concern across reviews. Consistent complaints about "$80 a night is outrageous," "very expensive to say the least," "most expensive nightly rate," and "facilities and service do not reflect the price." Many compare unfavorably to Big4 parks offering more amenities at similar or lower prices. Families note "no pool or other entertainment" doesn't justify premium pricing. 35% price increases mentioned in recent reviews.

Site Size (5/10) — Major weakness with overwhelming negative feedback. Reviews repeatedly describe "sites are really small & close together," "crammed in like sardines," "packed in tight," "everyone is squashed in," "way too small," and "sites so tiny." Multiple stories of difficulty maneuvering larger vans, needing neighbors to move vehicles, and vans not fitting allocated sites. Corner sites and waterfront sites particularly tight.

Sentiment (7/10) — Polarized responses. Strong loyalty from long-term visitors ("coming for nearly 20 years," "we keep coming back year after year") contrasts sharply with recent negative experiences citing poor customer service, rigid policies, and management changes. Common phrases: "will never visit this park again," "won't be returning," alongside "can't wait to come back." Many loyal families report declining to return after recent management changes.

Location (10/10) — Universally praised as exceptional. Reviews consistently highlight "perfect location," "location you can't beat," "one of the best caravan parks in Australia by miles," "walking distance to everything," "shops over the road," "cafes, restaurants just outside," and "close to Maroochydore CBD." Direct beach and river access plus proximity to Sunshine Plaza, Cotton Tree village, and local amenities earn full marks.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of on-site entertainment infrastructure (no pool, playground, or kids facilities) at premium pricing - families paying $76-84/night expect more amenities than just location.
2. Severely cramped sites described as "sardine-like" with multiple documented cases of caravans unable to fit allocated sites, creating stressful arrival experiences and safety concerns with children on bikes.
3. Inconsistent and often poor customer service with rigid cancellation policies, staff described as "rude," "unfriendly," "condescending," unresponsive communications, and lack of flexibility even for medical emergencies or extreme weather - creating significant negative sentiment despite excellent location.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Perfect location you have everything at your fingertips," "sites are really small & close together & completely jammed packed in," "$80 a night is outrageous," "no pool or other entertainment," "amenities are spotless," "crammed in like sardines," "staff absolutely rude," "best of both worlds access to river and ocean," "location you can't beat," "will never visit this park again"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with direct access to Coolum's patrolled beach, dog-friendly beach zones, and walking distance to shops, Coles, restaurants, and surf club - universally praised as the park's strongest asset Consistently clean and well-maintained amenities with most reviews describing facilities as 'super clean,' 'spotless,' and 'always serviced,' demonstrating strong operational standards Outstanding natural environment backing onto beautiful sandy beach with multiple walking paths, providing an ideal coastal setting for families who prioritize beach access over manufactured entertainment
rationale_honourable: Coolum Beach Holiday Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its exceptional beachfront location on the Sunshine Coast, with direct access to patrolled swimming areas and dog-friendly beach zones. Families appreciate the spotless amenities, well-maintained grounds, and the convenience of walking to shops, cafes, and the surf club. While the park lacks pools, playgrounds, and organized kids' activities, the natural beach environment and proximity to everything needed makes it a solid choice for families who prioritize location and beach access over on-site entertainment facilities.
water_fun: No pool; beach access only available
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment facilities
pet_detail: Dog-friendly sites available (approximately 113 sites); $5 per night per dog fee applies; on-leash beach access directly from park
best_for: Beach-loving families with young children who want direct beach access, proximity to patrolled swim areas, and easy walking distance to shops and cafes, but don't require on-site pools or playgrounds
executive_summary:
PARK: Coolum Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 173

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews consistently note there are no on-site kids facilities, playgrounds, pools, waterparks, or organized activities; one reviewer stated "no pool or kids playground you have to go out of the park" and another noted "park does not offer any extra touches like evening activities"; families must rely entirely on the beach and external attractions for entertainment.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural environment with direct beachfront access; reviews praise "right on the beach," "beautiful long sandy beach," "patrolled swim areas very close by," and "dog friendly beach" access; the park backs onto Coolum's main beach with multiple walking paths; loses points only for limited natural screening between sites.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Strong performance with most reviews praising "super clean" facilities, "spotless" amenities, "very clean and tidy," and "amenities always clean and serviced"; a few recent reviews mention occasional maintenance issues like broken lights and inconsistent cleaning frequency, with one noting "WCs should be cleaned twice a day."

Value (8/15) — Significant concern across reviews; repeatedly described as "overpriced," "way too expensive," "daylight robbery," with specific complaints about $75-$90+ per night for powered sites, $5 per dog per night fees, $10 tractor fees, and $6 washing machine charges; multiple long-term guests mention leaving due to price increases of 30%+; many feel the lack of facilities doesn't justify premium pricing.

Site Size (5/10) — Major weakness with frequent complaints about "sites are too small for large vans," "very tight," "cramped," "almost inaccessible," and requiring paid tractor assistance; some sites described as "width of a footpath"; premium dune sites receive better feedback for space, but standard sites are consistently criticized as inadequate for modern caravans.

Sentiment (8/10) — Mixed but leaning positive; many loyal repeat visitors over 10-30 years love the location, but recent sentiment shows declining satisfaction due to pricing and customer service issues; positive comments about "favourite caravan park" and "will definitely be returning" are balanced by "won't be going back" and "never again" from previously loyal guests.

Location (10/10) — Near-perfect score with universal praise for "best location," "walking distance to everything," "across the road to shops, Coles, restaurants," "minutes to surf club," and "no need to drive anywhere"; beachfront position and town convenience are the park's strongest assets.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities (no pool, playground, kids club, or organized activities) severely limits appeal for families with young children seeking on-site activities.
2. Persistent customer service issues with multiple reviews citing "very rude staff," "unhelpful reception," "abusive and intolerant," poor communication, unreturned calls, and inflexible policies around bookings and health emergencies.
3. Site size and accessibility problems make the park unsuitable for many modern caravans, with paid tractor service required for numerous sites and frequent complaints about cramped conditions and difficult maneuvering.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best location for a van park," "right on the beach," "facilities were super clean," "walking distance to shops and surf club," "no pool or kids playground," "sites are too small for large vans," "staff are very rude," "way overpriced," "require tractor assistance," "dog friendly beach access," "highest we've ever paid," "nothing special about the caravan park other than location"

rationale_top3: Outstanding beachside location with walking distance to beach, cafes, park and dog-friendly beach access - the park's greatest strength with families consistently praising the 'beautiful beach on the sunny coast' and '2 minutes from the beach' convenience Exceptional nature score (18/20) reflecting the natural coastal setting, proximity to Maroochy River, and off-leash dog beach areas that create an ideal environment for outdoor-focused families Generally spacious, well-kept sites with amenities often described as 'the cleanest I've experienced at a van park ever' and 'impeccably clean', showing strong facility maintenance in core areas
rationale_honourable: Mudjimba Beach Holiday Park achieves Silver classification (68/100) through its premium beachside location and natural setting, with families returning 'year after year' for the unbeatable beach access and dog-friendly policies. However, significant issues prevent Gold status: severe cleanliness inconsistency (spotless amenities versus 'filthy' camp kitchens with cockroaches and mold), persistent reception staff attitude problems described as 'rude' and 'aggressive', minimal on-site kids entertainment (no playground on property, basic pool only), and premium pricing ($80-$100/night) that families feel doesn't match the value delivered compared to competitor parks with better facilities.
kids_play: Playground across road near beach, minimal on-site
pet_detail: Dog friendly in designated sections ($5 per dog per night), separate pet-free area available, cats not permitted, dog beach nearby
best_for: This park is best for families who value prime beachside location and natural coastal surroundings over on-site facilities, and who don't mind paying higher prices for the convenience of beach access and dog-friendly policies.
executive_summary:
PARK: Mudjimba Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-09
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 143

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Minimal on-site kids entertainment noted; families mention 'playground across the road' near beach and pool available but 'no improvements in the park or any new facilities' with complaints about lack of 'playgrounds for kids and water activities' compared to BIG 4 parks. Pool described as basic with 170cm depth, primarily used for cooling off rather than entertainment.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting with 'walking distance to beach, cafes and park', 'beach is just down the road', '2 minutes from the beach', 'beautiful beach on the sunny coast', and 'dog friendly beach just across the road'. Park has shade from paperbark trees though this creates leaf debris. Close to Maroochy River (2-3km) and off-leash dog beach areas.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Highly inconsistent feedback; amenities frequently praised as 'the cleanest I've experienced at a van park ever', 'impeccably clean', 'spotless and new', but camp kitchens heavily criticized with 'filthy kitchen by the pool - dirty, moldy, smelling', 'cockroach running under the fridge', 'floor tiles need to be scrubbed as soap scum is building up'. Grounds maintenance issues with leaves and debris on sites upon arrival.

Value (8/15) — Significant concerns about pricing with numerous mentions of '$80-$100 a night' being 'over the top', 'unethically high prices', 'way overpriced for quality', '26% price increase' without improvements, '$5 per dog per night' fee seen as 'money grab'. Some acknowledge prices 'on par with other options' on Sunshine Coast but many felt poor value compared to what's offered.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive with 'spacious sites', 'big sites', 'generous and well kept', 'larger than normal sites', 'plenty of space and drive through site'. However some complaints about 'dirt sites' instead of advertised grass, uneven sites, and 'sites are quite close together'. Some tent campers placed 'down the back' away from facilities.

Sentiment (7/10) — Mixed sentiment; many loyal returners who 'come back year after year' and 'love everything about this place', but significantly undermined by repeated complaints about 'rude' reception staff with 'attitude adjustment' needed, described as 'rudest woman', 'aggressive', and 'unfriendly'. Management issues with refunds, communication, and flexibility. Positive mentions of specific helpful staff (Hasini, Anna) but pattern of poor customer service interactions.

Location (8/10) — Excellent beachside location 'walking distance to beach, cafes and park', 'close to coffee shops, eateries', '5 minutes drive to Coles', 'great for visiting the zoo', 'close enough to access all of the Sunshine Coast'. Airport proximity noted with 'planes flying low all through the day' but 'no late planes' and 'quiet at night'. 'Great community feel' in small surf village setting.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe cleanliness inconsistency - while amenity blocks are praised, camp kitchens repeatedly described as 'filthy' with cockroaches, mold, broken equipment, and inadequate responses to complaints, creating hygiene concerns for families.
2. Persistent reception staff attitude problems with multiple reviews over 3 years citing 'rude', 'aggressive', 'unfriendly' interactions, poor refund handling, and making guests feel unwelcome, significantly impacting the family holiday experience.
3. Minimal on-site kids entertainment with no playground on property, basic pool only, no kids activities or programs, and premium pricing ($80-$100/night) that families expect would include better facilities and value for children.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"walking distance to beach, cafes and park", "the cleanest amenities I've experienced at a van park ever", "woman in reception needs an attitude adjustment", "filthy kitchen by the pool", "$80 for a dirt site is over the top", "come back year after year", "playground across the road for the kids", "way overpriced for quality of the sites"

rationale_top3: Exceptional natural setting with wooded environment, central lagoon/pond featuring ducks, turtles, water dragons, and eels that children love feeding - consistently praised as the park's standout feature Outstanding location just 500m from Australia Zoo with walking distance to Landsborough town, 20 minutes to beaches, and convenient access to Glasshouse Mountains and hinterland attractions Spacious powered sites with drive-through options and generally clean amenities, plus helpful staff creating a comfortable base for Sunshine Coast exploration
rationale_honourable: Landsborough Holiday Park earns its Silver rating primarily through its exceptional natural setting with pond, wildlife, and proximity to Australia Zoo. Families consistently praise the tranquil wooded environment where kids can observe ducks, turtles, water dragons, and abundant birdlife. The park's clean amenities, spacious powered sites, and helpful staff create a solid base for visiting Sunshine Coast attractions, though limited on-site entertainment and some maintenance issues prevent a higher score.
water_fun: Small pool needing upgrade, not main attraction
kids_play: No playground; nature-based entertainment with pond wildlife
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with pets welcomed, multiple reviews confirm dogs allowed on sites
best_for: This park best suits families with young children who love wildlife and nature-based activities, planning day trips to Australia Zoo and Sunshine Coast attractions rather than seeking on-site entertainment facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Landsborough Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 123

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — No playground facilities and very small, dated pool requiring upgrade. Entertainment comes entirely from natural surroundings: duck pond with turtles, water dragons, eels, and abundant birdlife that kids enjoy feeding. One family noted staff created cricket pitch for kids' carnival. Limited structured activities but shuttle bus to Australia Zoo (500m away) mentioned positively.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting with wooded area, lagoon/pond central feature, little creek running behind sites. Families loved feeding ducks and wildlife, peaceful setting amongst trees, healthy birdlife. Close to Glasshouse Mountains and hinterland villages. Natural surroundings consistently praised as highlight, though some mentioned pond smell and mosquitoes near water.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Mixed reports with recent reviews showing decline. Many praised "beautiful and clean" facilities, "immaculate amenities", "SO CLEAN" bathrooms. However, concerning reports of mouldy showers, filthy cleaning equipment, dust in cabins, and facilities needing deep clean. Amenities described as needing refurbishment and "run down" by multiple recent guests.

Value (10/15) — Pricing concerns evident with unpowered sites at $55 deemed "completely overpriced", powered sites $48 "a bit too expensive". Several families felt disappointed by what they received versus cost. Positive value mentions for cabin stays and good amenities. Australia Zoo 10% discount offered. Overall mixed with more recent reviews trending negative on value.

Site Size (8/10) — Powered sites consistently described as "spacious", "generous", "good sized" with room for larger vans. Drive-through sites available. However, some powered sites noted as "small" and unpowered sites described as inadequate muddy parking slots. Gravel/road-base surfaces not grass, making tent camping difficult.

Sentiment (7/10) — Positive repeat intention from many guests but significant negative experiences impact score. Permanent residents caused friction with families, verbal abuse reported, kids told to leave areas. Service issues with power failures, refund problems, poor communication. Recent management changes noted. Many 5-star reviews but critical 1-2 star reviews from families highlight serious concerns.

Location (10/10) — Excellent location scores with Australia Zoo 4-5 minutes away (500m), walking distance to Landsborough town and shops, 20 minutes to beaches in either direction, close to Caloundra, Montville, Maleny, and Glasshouse Mountains. Train station nearby. Convenience for Sunshine Coast exploration consistently praised, though traffic noise from major intersection mentioned.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No playground or kids' entertainment facilities with a very small, dated pool needing urgent renovation – families with active children found it "useless as a holiday park" with nothing for kids to do on-site.
2. Significant permanent resident presence (majority of park) creating friction with holidaying families – children told they weren't welcome in certain areas, complaints made about normal kids' play, making families feel uncomfortable and unable to enjoy their stay.
3. Cleanliness standards declining with recent reports of mouldy showers, dirty facilities, dust in cabins, malfunctioning equipment, and amenities needing refurbishment – inconsistent maintenance affecting guest experience.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids loved the ducks and turtles in the pond", "Australia Zoo is only 500 metres down the road", "Pool super small and no play ground area for kids", "permanent residents ruined this one for us", "Beautiful clean spacious amenities", "amenities could do with refurbishment", "peaceful setting amongst the trees", "felt like we were holidaying in a retirement village", "lots of wildlife to see in the pond", "close to everything the coast has to offer"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachside location with 5-minute walk to patrolled beach, shops, restaurants and Mooloolaba attractions - rated 10/10 for location being 'one of the best on the Sunshine Coast' Consistently spotlessly clean amenities and well-maintained grounds scoring 14/15 for cleanliness, though recent reviews note some maintenance decline with broken equipment and less frequent grounds care Small, secure 60-site family-oriented park encouraging bikes, scooters and skateboards with concrete slabs and adequate site spacing, though completely lacking pool or on-site entertainment facilities
rationale_honourable: Mooloolaba Beach Holiday Park delivers on its prime beachside location with walking access to Mooloolaba's attractions, patrolled beach, cafes and shops. The park consistently receives praise for spotlessly clean amenities and well-maintained grounds, though families should note the absence of a pool and limited on-site entertainment. While the small 60-site park offers security, concrete slabs and basic facilities, value concerns and increasing prices are frequently mentioned by returning guests.
water_fun: No pool; beach access via gates
kids_play: Bikes and scooters encouraged; nearby attractions
best_for: Families with active children who prioritize beach access, clean facilities and walking distance to Mooloolaba's attractions over on-site water features and entertainment
executive_summary:
PARK: Mooloolaba Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 39

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — No pool or dedicated kids entertainment facilities mentioned; families note park encourages bikes, scooters and skateboards during the day with nearby attractions like Sea Life, Aquaduck, wharf and fishing providing off-site activities for children during school holidays.
Nature (18/20) — Outstanding natural environment with direct beach access via two security gates, walking distance to patrolled swimming beach, Alexandra Heads and the Spit; location described as "smack bang in the middle of beautiful Mooloolaba" with beach being the primary natural drawcard.
Cleanliness (14/15) — Consistently praised as "spotlessly clean" and "perfectly clean" with "excellent clean amenities"; recent reviews note some maintenance decline with trees needing trimming, broken dryers for 4 weeks, and grounds maintenance becoming less frequent despite price increases.
Value (8/15) — Significant concern across reviews with prices described as "absolute daylight robbery," "comparably expensive," and "too expensive"; rates of $90-95 per night seen as high for a "very basic park" with no pool, though some guests feel location balances cost.
Site Size (7/10) — Small 60-site park with concrete slabs providing "ample room on the provided slab and single car parking"; described as compact but adequate with good site spacing though specific size dimensions not detailed in reviews.
Sentiment (8/10) — Mixed sentiment with many 5-star reviews praising location and cleanliness but recent reviews show declining satisfaction; repeat visitors express disappointment with service decline, pricing policies, and maintenance issues causing some to seek alternative parks.
Location (10/10) — Perfect score for "one of the best locations on the Sunshine Coast" with 5-minute walk to beach, shops, restaurants, cafes, Coles supermarket, and attractions; only negative is road noise on roadside sites and Friday night traffic.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of pool or water play facilities for a beach holiday park, with no on-site kids entertainment programs or activities to occupy young children on rainy days or hot afternoons.
2. Value concerns dominate recent reviews with prices of $90-95 per night seen as excessive for basic facilities, coupled with aggressive deposit policies (full week payment) and declining service standards despite price increases.
3. Maintenance and service quality declining noticeably in recent reviews including broken dryers for 4 weeks, infrequent grounds care, poor camp kitchen setup with no basic amenities, and customer service issues with unanswered phone calls.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean", "5 minute stroll to the beach", "family oriented park", "no pool but very close to beach", "comparably expensive", "scooters, bikes and skateboards are encouraged", "security patrols on site during busy periods", "one of the best locations on the Sunshine Coast"

rationale_top3: Complete absence of kids entertainment facilities makes this unsuitable for families with young children, resulting in explicit warnings from family reviewers Inconsistent and declining cleanliness standards with multiple reports of disgusting amenities, missed cleaning schedules, pest infestations in cabins, and poor maintenance Extremely poor value proposition charging premium beachfront rates ($70-80/night) for run-down, aging facilities with minimal amenities that don't justify the cost
rationale_honourable: Maroochydore Beach Holiday Park benefits enormously from its outstanding beachfront location with direct private access, making it incredibly convenient for families who prioritise beach proximity above all else. The natural coastal environment is beautiful and the location near Cotton Tree and Mooloolaba offers excellent walkability to cafes, shops and attractions. However, the park falls short on family-specific facilities with no kids entertainment, inconsistent cleanliness standards, cramped sites, and pricing that doesn't reflect the aging infrastructure and minimal amenities provided.
water_fun: No pool or water facilities mentioned
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment available
pet_detail: Dogs allowed on-lead for $5 extra per night; park and beach are dog-friendly but must remain on lead
best_for: This park suits couples and older travellers who value prime beachfront location over facilities and can tolerate basic, aging amenities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Maroochydore Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-04-10
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 53

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — Multiple families explicitly stated there is "nothing kid friendly or anything for kids to do" and "Very little for children," with the only activity being beach access. This is a critical deficiency for family holiday parks.

Nature (18/20) — The park's strongest asset is its beachfront location with "private access to the beach," described as "literally on the beach through an access gate in 1 minute." The natural coastal environment and proximity to patrolled beaches earned consistent praise.

Cleanliness (8/15) — Reviews show inconsistent standards, with some praising "extra ordinarily clean" facilities while recent reviews report "amenities were disgusting," "showers a disgusting mess," amenities not cleaned for days, and cabins "infested with cockroaches and ants."

Value (6/15) — Overwhelmingly negative feedback on pricing: "Very expensive for what you get," "NOT worth 80$ per night," "Very overpriced," with costs at $68-$80 per night for basic, aging facilities that don't match the premium charged.

Site Size (5/10) — Consistent complaints about "EXTREAMLY tight and squishy" sites with poor site allocation practices, though some beach-side sites received positive mentions for adequate space.

Sentiment (6/10) — Mixed sentiment with polarised reviews; some long-term guests loved it and rebooked, but many families with children were deeply disappointed and explicitly warned others not to visit.

Location (10/10) — Universal praise for "prime location" right on the beach, walking distance to Cotton Tree, Mooloolaba, surf clubs, shops, restaurants, and cafes, with bus access directly outside.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of kids entertainment facilities makes this unsuitable for families with young children, resulting in explicit warnings from family reviewers
2. Inconsistent and declining cleanliness standards with multiple reports of disgusting amenities, missed cleaning schedules, pest infestations in cabins, and poor maintenance
3. Extremely poor value proposition charging premium beachfront rates ($70-80/night) for run-down, aging facilities with minimal amenities that don't justify the cost

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"nothing kid friendly or anything for kids to do," "amenities were disgusting," "$80 per night the park is run down," "sites are also EXTREAMLY tight and squishy," "literally on the beach through an access gate in 1 minute," "Very expensive for what you get," "cabin infested with cockroaches and ants," "Very little for children"
